sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Verrica Pharmaceuticals Q4 Loss Reignites Debate On Path To Sustainable Profitability
feed.summary: Verrica Pharmaceuticals reported a Q4 basic EPS loss of US$0.57, despite revenue growth throughout 2025, leading to renewed debate on its path to sustainable profitability. The company's trailing twelve-month revenue was US$35.6 million with a net loss of US$17.9 million, highlighting that increased sales have not yet offset operating costs. While growth forecasts are strong, concerns remain over deepening losses, a short cash runway, and shareholder dilution.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: Two-time Olympic Champions Have a Bell Moment on the Trading Floor
feed.summary: The NYSE daily pre-market update on March 13th features market insights, an interview with Sean Bill of Bitcoin Standard Treasury Company, and a segment on HSBC SVNS New York with rugby executive Alan Gilpin and retired player Naya Tapper. Olympic figure skating champions Madison Chock and Evan Bates also enjoyed a Bell Moment after their success at the Milan Cortina Winter Games. The trading day includes the opening bell by ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott and the closing bell by EltaMD Skin Care.

feed.title: Tyler Technologies Launches New $200 Million Buyback Plan
feed.summary: Tyler Technologies (TYL) has announced a new Rule 10b5-1 trading plan to repurchase up to $200 million of its common stock between March 16 and April 30, 2026. This plan operates under a larger $1 billion share repurchase authorization launched in February 2026, with the company currently having $734.4 million in remaining capacity. The repurchases will be funded by cash on hand and borrowings, providing a transparent and compliant method for returning capital to shareholders.

feed.title: UBS reiterates Abbott Labs stock rating on structural heart upside
feed.summary: UBS has reiterated a Buy rating and a $158.00 price target for Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) due to the anticipated upside from its structural heart business. The firm expects a positive CHAMPION readout by the end of March, which should be presented at ACC on March 28. Despite a recent stock decline driven by a disappointing Q4 and 2026 guidance, UBS maintains conviction in the sustainability of double-digit Medical Device growth, viewing Structural Heart as an underappreciated contributor.

feed.title: Xcel Energy plans for possible power shutoffs in Colorado this weekend
feed.summary: Xcel Energy is planning possible Public Safety Power Shutoffs (PSPS) in Boulder and Jefferson counties, Colorado, starting Saturday, March 14, around 2 p.m., due to high winds and critical wildfire danger. Although weather conditions are expected to improve later that day, strong winds are forecast to continue through the weekend, potentially causing further disruptions for customers already affected by earlier outages. Xcel Energy advises customers to prepare for potential outages, especially those relying on medical equipment.

feed.title: EV Investors Hoping for a Rebound Could Be Left Waiting
feed.summary: U.S. electric vehicle (EV) registrations declined for the first time in a decade last year, dropping 0.4% overall and dramatically by 48% in December due to the expiration of federal tax credits. While Tesla maintains a significant market share, its lead is shrinking, and most automakers, including Ford, saw substantial declines, though GM's Cadillac was a rare bright spot. Despite continued improvements in charging infrastructure and narrowing price gaps, analysts suggest investors should not expect a quick rebound in EV sales, instead anticipating a gradual increase.

feed.title: Uber's Vegas Robotaxis Still Need A Human Watching The Road
feed.summary: Uber has launched an all-electric robotaxi service in Las Vegas through a partnership with Motional. While the Motional IONIQ 5 robotaxi is designed for driverless operations and is a SAE Level 4 autonomous vehicle, it currently still requires a human operator to monitor the road. This initiative is part of Uber's broader strategy to expand its autonomous vehicle offerings and includes global partnerships with Wayve and Nissan for robotaxis in Tokyo, and Zoox in the U.S.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts PT on Targa Resources to $264 From $248, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for Targa Resources (TRGP) to $264, up from the previous $248. The firm reiterated an Overweight rating on the stock.

feed.title: Coca-Cola UNITED lists longtime campus for sale
feed.summary: Coca-Cola UNITED has put its longtime East Lake Boulevard campus in Birmingham up for sale. This move is part of a larger $330 million investment by the company, which is expected to retain over 750 jobs. The campus features 350,000 square feet of office and industrial space.

feed.title: MS Now, Golf Channel, CNBC, & More Plan to Make Half of Their Revenue From Non-Cable TV Sources After Comcast Spun Them Off This Year
feed.summary: Versant Media Group, the independent media company spun off from Comcast, aims for 50% of its $6.6 billion 2026 revenue to come from non-cable TV sources. This strategy, driven by cord-cutting trends, involves leveraging digital assets like GolfNow and Fandango, strategic acquisitions such as Indy Cinema Group and Free TV Networks, and direct-to-consumer initiatives for brands like MS Now and CNBC. The company seeks to balance legacy strengths with digital growth to ensure sustainability in an evolving media landscape.

feed.title: Kroger pharmacies add Zepbound KwikPen as GLP-1 access expands
feed.summary: Kroger pharmacies are now offering the Zepbound KwikPen, a new multi-dose delivery method for Eli Lilly's weight-management therapy, broadening access to GLP-1 obesity treatments. Kroger will honor manufacturer-sponsored savings programs like Lilly’s KwikPen Self-Pay Savings Card, making the product more affordable for eligible self-pay patients at a retail pharmacy for the first time. Beyond dispensing, Kroger pharmacies will provide comprehensive support including pharmacist counseling, virtual nutrition care, and integrated services with The Little Clinic, connecting patients with dietary guidance and in-store food choices.

feed.title: Summit Royalties boost portfolio with royalty interest in Newmont's Saddle North deposit
feed.summary: Summit Royalties has acquired a 1% Net Smelter Return (NSR) royalty on Newmont Corporation's Saddle North deposit for C$5 million in an all-stock transaction. CEO Drew Clark highlighted the significance of Saddle North, which contains substantial gold and copper, positioning it as a long-term asset to Summit's cash-flow positive and expanding precious metals royalty portfolio. The company is actively pursuing accretive acquisitions and has multiple operational and development catalysts across its assets, including Pitangui and Zancudo projects.

feed.title: Starbucks union says it’s working with company to revive contract talks
feed.summary: Starbucks Workers United announced it has made a new comprehensive contract proposal and is in discussions with Starbucks to restart negotiations. This development follows a period of stalled talks and a recent strike, with the union proposing a US$17 minimum wage, 4% annual raises, and specific staffing requirements. The two parties have yet to reach a collective bargaining agreement since the first Starbucks café unionized over four years ago.

feed.title: Blecharczyk reports multiple ABNB share sales (NASDAQ: ABNB)
feed.summary: Nathan Blecharczyk, co-founder of Airbnb (NASDAQ: ABNB), and affiliated trusts reported multiple sales of ABNB Class A shares between December 19, 2025, and February 27, 2026. These routine insider dispositions include various transactions, such as the sale of 7,692 shares for over $1 million by the Nathan Blecharczyk 2020 Remainder Trust. The SEC Form 144 filing provides details on the number of shares sold and gross proceeds for each transaction, but does not include information on trading plans or tax-withholding.

feed.title: A Look At DuPont (DD) Valuation After Earnings Beat And Water Technology Updates
feed.summary: DuPont de Nemours (DD) has garnered attention with its better-than-expected Q4 2025 results and optimistic 2026 guidance, alongside advancements in its water technology portfolio. Despite recent short-term share price declines, the stock shows positive momentum over 90 days and a year. Currently trading at a discount to analyst targets and its intrinsic value, the article explores whether this represents a genuine mispricing or if the market has already factored in future growth.

feed.title: Qualcomm Qt Alliance Highlights Edge AI Push In Smart Factories
feed.summary: Qualcomm and Qt Group have partnered to accelerate Edge AI device development for smart factories, leveraging a pre-optimized Qt framework for Qualcomm Dragonwing IQ processors to simplify AI deployment in industrial settings. This collaboration aims to diversify Qualcomm's business beyond smartphones into industrial automation, focusing on use cases like predictive maintenance and voice-activated control. The partnership seeks to reduce friction for factory software teams building AI applications, positioning Qualcomm competitively against other chip players in the Edge AI market, despite potential slow adoption by industrial customers.

feed.title: The Nitrogen King: Why CF Industries is March 2026’s #1 S&P 500 Performer
feed.summary: CF Industries (NYSE: CF) has become the top-performing S&P 500 stock in March 2026, driven by geopolitical volatility impacting fertilizer exports through the Strait of Hormuz and a market shift towards "hard assets, low-carbon operations." The company, a pure-play nitrogen manufacturer, benefits from its cost advantage using North American natural gas and is strategically pivoting to blue ammonia production, attracting ESG-conscious institutional capital. Despite risks like operational reliability and natural gas price volatility, CF's strong financials, disciplined leadership, and role in the marine fuel transition position it favorably in the current market.

feed.title: Assessing WaFd (WAFD) Valuation After Erin Hill Joins The Board And Audit Committee
feed.summary: WaFd (WAFD) recently appointed Erin Hill to its board and Audit Committee, bringing significant financial services experience. The company's stock has seen a 6.02% 30-day return but a 3.36% year-to-date decline, though its 1-year total shareholder return is 15.77%. While the market generally views WAFD as 20% overvalued with a $31 fair value, Simply Wall St's DCF model suggests a fair value of $38.11, implying potential upside.

feed.title: Research Alert: Wheaton Precious Metals Posts Record Q4, Announces $4.3b Antamina Expansion
feed.summary: MT Newswires reports that Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) posted record Q4 results and announced a $4.3 billion expansion for Antamina. Concurrently, Unity Software (U.US) reported Q4 revenue of $609 million, exceeding estimates, despite a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: HP's ink-blocking firmware may violate new global sustainability rules
feed.summary: HP's firmware updates, which block third-party ink cartridges, are again under scrutiny, this time for potentially violating the new EPEAT 2.0 global sustainability standard. The International Imaging Technology Council (Int'l ITC) argues that HP's latest firmware conflicts with EPEAT 2.0's requirements, which explicitly forbid such practices to encourage component reuse. This issue highlights a conflict between corporate firmware control and evolving sustainability policies in the printing industry.

feed.title: Amex secretly axes most used perk after shoppers discovered $200 ‘fee credit’ loophole
feed.summary: American Express is facing backlash from customers after it secretly closed a credit card loophole that allowed Platinum cardholders to trigger their $200 annual airline incidental credit by depositing funds into United Airlines’ TravelBank. This loophole was popular because it provided a more flexible $200 flight credit instead of restricting it to incidental charges like baggage fees. Amex clarified that TravelBank purchases are considered gift cards and thus are not eligible for incidental fee credits under their terms, leading many users to feel the card has lost significant value.

feed.title: General Mills unveils new soft baked bars
feed.summary: General Mills has introduced two new lines of soft baked bars under its Nature Valley brand. These include the Nature Valley soft baked PB&J bars, available in peanut butter with strawberry or grape, and the Nature Valley Kids soft baked bars, which offer 5 grams of protein in flavors like peanut butter brownie, chocolate chip, and s'mores. The new products aim to reimagine classic flavors into kid-approved formats.

feed.title: Copart Inc stock hits 52-week low at $33.81 as market challenges persist
feed.summary: Copart Inc (CPRT) stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $33.81, experiencing a 35% decline over the past year due to persistent market challenges. Despite this, InvestingPro analysis indicates that the stock may be undervalued with a fair value of $38.06 and maintains a strong financial health rating with more cash than debt. In other news, Copart recently secured a new $1.25 billion unsecured senior revolving credit agreement to replace its previous facility, providing significant financial flexibility.

feed.title: McDonald’s Stock (MCD) Moves Past the Defensive Label. Here’s Why I’m Bullish
feed.summary: McDonald's (MCD) is evolving beyond a purely defensive stock due to its aggressive growth strategy. This includes the fastest restaurant expansion in its history, aiming for 50,000 locations by 2027, significant digital engagement with its MyMcDonald's Rewards program, and the implementation of AI and automation for operational efficiency. The company also continues to drive sales through menu innovation and value offerings, leading to a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Investigates Western Alliance Bancorporation
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has launched an investigation into Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE: WAL) for potential securities fraud, following a significant 10.81% drop in the company's stock price on October 16, 2025. This decline occurred after the bank revealed issues with a credit relationship, including allegations of fraud and breached loan agreements. The firm is considering a class action lawsuit to help investors recover losses.

feed.title: Micron Faces Upcycle as Analysts Boost Price Targets
feed.summary: Micron Technology Inc. (NASDAQ: MU) stocks have risen over 5.72%, driven by several analysts increasing their price targets, signaling strong market sentiment and an anticipated upcycle. Firms like Aletheia, Stifel, Citi, Susquehanna, and UBS have boosted targets due to high demand for AI-optimized memory, better-than-expected HBM and DDR5 pricing, and robust data center demand. Micron's strategic moves, such as investments in new facilities and product developments, alongside resilient financial fundamentals, position it strongly to capitalize on future technological advancements despite potential market volatility.

feed.title: Western Digital Surges Forward Amid Dynamic Industry Shifts
feed.summary: Western Digital's stock has risen by 6.2% following positive responses to its strategic mergers and acquisitions, including a bold multi-year growth plan targeting over $20 EPS, optimistic profit margin projections, and a price target upgrade to $360 from Arete. The company is unifying its brand under G-DRIVE, phasing out SanDisk Professional, and managing its capital effectively through maneuvers like a secondary public offering of Sandisk shares. These strategic moves and strong financial health position Western Digital for sustained growth in a dynamic industry.

feed.title: Plus-size traveler blasts Southwest for gate agent’s discrimination: ‘Guess what, my fat ass did fit in the seat’
feed.summary: A plus-size Southwest Airlines passenger, Erika, claims she was discriminated against by a gate agent who suggested she purchase an extra seat for her flight to Las Vegas. Erika, who identifies as plus-size, asserted she had never needed an extra seat and was shocked when the agent cited "safety of other passengers." The incident has sparked outrage on social media, with many condemning Southwest's controversial "Customer of Size" policy, which mandates passengers encroaching on neighboring seats to buy an additional one.

feed.title: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Adobe, Micron, Western Digital, Coinbase, Ulta Beauty, CF Industries, and More
feed.summary: Stocks, including Adobe, Micron, Western Digital, Coinbase, Ulta Beauty, and CF Industries, showed movement on Friday. The market experienced an upturn as oil prices dropped and new personal consumption expenditures data indicated a slight increase in inflation during February. This article highlights the key movers of the day amidst these economic indicators.

feed.title: PEG Stock Downgraded by Ladenburg Thalmann with Price Target Low
feed.summary: Ladenburg Thalmann downgraded Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) from 'Buy' to 'Neutral' and lowered its price target from $87.50 to $84.50, citing a recalibration of future performance expectations. This comes amidst varied analyst activity, with some firms raising price targets and others adjusting ratings. Despite the downgrade, analysts on average project an "Outperform" status for PEG, with an average price target of $90.36, suggesting a potential upside from its current price.

feed.title: Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) Boosts Dividend by 18.2%
feed.summary: Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) has increased its quarterly dividend by 18.2% to $0.195 per share, payable on April 10 to shareholders of record by March 31. The company also reported strong financial results, with non-GAAP EPS of $1.22 and revenue of $864.71 million, both exceeding expectations. Despite strong financial health and a debt-free balance sheet, valuation metrics suggest the stock may be overvalued compared to historical norms, though analyst sentiment remains positive.

feed.title: The AI Control Tower: ServiceNow (NOW) Surges 16% as Autonomous Roaming Redefines Enterprise Workflows
feed.summary: ServiceNow (NOW) has seen a 16% surge in its stock over the past month, driven by its transformation into an autonomous AI orchestrator and a groundbreaking partnership with NTT DOCOMO and StarHub to solve autonomous roaming. CEO Bill McDermott's $3 million share purchase signals strong confidence in the company's AI potential. ServiceNow's "AI Control Tower" and "Autonomous Action" capabilities are solidifying its position as a leader in enterprise AI orchestration, moving beyond basic GenAI applications.

feed.title: Missile Solutions chief at L3Harris to speak at J.P. Morgan event
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies announced that Ken Bedingfield, President of Missile Solutions, will speak at the J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference on Wednesday, March 18, at 12:55 p.m. ET. The presentation will be streamed live on L3Harris.com, with a recording available afterward. L3Harris is a defense tech company providing end-to-end technology solutions across various domains for national security.

feed.title: International Paper Discloses Share Purchases by Non-Executive Director
feed.summary: International Paper announced that non-executive director Anders Gustafsson purchased additional common shares on the NYSE on March 11 and 12, 2026. These transactions, priced in the high-$30 range, were disclosed under EU Market Abuse Regulation requirements, signifying insider alignment with shareholder interests. International Paper is a global leader in sustainable packaging solutions, listed on both the New York and London Stock Exchanges.

feed.title: KNX Rating Upgraded to 'Buy' by Citigroup, Target Price Unchanged at $64 | KNX Stock News
feed.summary: Citigroup has upgraded Knight-Swift (KNX) to a 'Buy' rating from 'Neutral', maintaining its price target at $64 USD. This upgrade reflects a positive shift in the analyst's outlook for the largest full-truckload carrier in the US. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $63.85 for KNX, implying a 22.76% upside from its current price.

feed.title: How Is Fortive's Stock Performance Compared to Other Infrastructure Development Stocks?
feed.summary: Fortive Corporation (FTV), an industrial technology company, has significantly underperformed the Global X U.S. Infrastructure Development ETF (PAVE) and Coherent Corp. (COHR) over the past year. While PAVE gained 33.1% and COHR surged 258.4%, FTV declined by 25.7%. Analysts have a "Hold" rating on Fortive's stock, projecting a modest upside.

feed.title: Is Cardinal Health Stock Outperforming the Dow?
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (CAH) has demonstrated strong stock performance, significantly outperforming the Dow Jones Industrials Average (DOWI) year-to-date and over the past 52 weeks. The healthcare services company reported robust Q2 2026 results, with substantial increases in revenue and adjusted EPS, leading to a raised full-year guidance and a "Strong Buy" consensus among analysts. Despite a slight decline from its 52-week high, CAH's stock performance and positive outlook position it favorably in the market.

feed.title: Mercedes-Benz Group AG: Release according to Article 40, Section 1 of the WpHG [the German Securities Trading Act] with the objective of Europe-wide distribution
feed.summary: Mercedes-Benz Group AG has released a notification regarding major holdings, indicating that BlackRock, Inc. has crossed a voting rights threshold on March 10, 2026. BlackRock's total position in Mercedes-Benz Group AG is now 6.25%, comprising 6.11% voting rights attached to shares and 0.15% through instruments. The company also detailed the full chain of controlled undertakings under BlackRock, Inc.

feed.title: Uber relaunches Motional robotaxi service in Las Vegas
feed.summary: Uber is relaunching its robotaxi service in Las Vegas with Motional, a company backed by Hyundai. This partnership allows Uber riders to be matched with a Motional Ioniq 5 autonomous vehicle for UberX, Uber Electric, or Uber Comfort rides at no extra cost, with safety drivers initially present but expected to be removed by year-end. This is part of Uber's strategy to become an aggregator of robotaxi services, expanding its autonomous vehicle offerings in various cities globally.

feed.title: Ecolab Inc. Stock (ISIN: US2824031014) Faces Pressure Amid Energy Surcharge and Mixed Valuation Signals
feed.summary: Ecolab Inc. is implementing a 10-14% global energy surcharge starting April 1, 2026, to address rising input costs, while simultaneously providing robust 2026 guidance with projected 12-15% adjusted EPS growth. Despite a recent dip in share price after its last earnings report, analysts maintain a consensus target price suggesting significant upside potential. The company's business model, characterized by recurring revenues from essential water, hygiene, and energy solutions, aims to maintain strong margins amidst valuation debates and market caution.

feed.title: Chipotle’s Friday the 13th BOGO deal has one catch: You need a tattoo
feed.summary: Chipotle is offering a buy-one-get-one-free entrée deal for one hour on Friday, March 13th, for customers with any type of tattoo, whether permanent, temporary, or drawn on. The promotion, "tatted like a Chipotle bag," is inspired by Friday the 13th flash tattoo traditions and a popular internet meme. It will run from 3 to 4 p.m. local time at participating locations across multiple countries, requiring an in-restaurant purchase of an entrée of equal or greater value to receive the free item.

feed.title: Travelzoo Recognised with Three Gold Awards at the Canadian Online Publishing Awards: All You Need To Know
feed.summary: Travelzoo won three Gold Awards at the 17th Canadian Online Publishing Awards (COPAs), establishing itself as a leader in online travel media. The company was recognized for its "Top 20" newsletter and received awards for Best Travel Story and Best Photojournalism for its "Malta Moved Me" feature. This achievement highlights Travelzoo's consistent delivery of high-quality content and exclusive travel deals to its 30 million members.

feed.title: WAY SEC Filings - Waystar Holding Corp. 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive resource for Waystar Holding Corp. (WAY) SEC filings, including annual 10-K, quarterly 10-Q, and material event 8-K reports, alongside insider trading forms. Stock Titan offers direct access to these regulatory documents and AI-powered summaries to help investors analyze the financial condition, capital structure, and significant corporate events of the healthcare payment software company. Recent filings detail insider transactions, credit agreement amendments, and institutional ownership stakes.

feed.title: Private credit sell-off an ‘overreaction,’ Raymond James' Burdis says: CNBC interview
feed.summary: The article reports that Rayomnd James' Burdis believes the private credit sell-off is an "overreaction." He expects defaults in the private credit market to rise but argues that the asset class is still attractive due to its floating rate nature and collateral protection. He also highlighted the potential for market opportunities as institutional investors seek to rebalance their portfolios.

feed.title: Pleated Filters Market Is Going to Boom Rapidly | 3M Company, Parker Hannifin Corporation, Camfil AB
feed.summary: Coherent Market Insights has released a new research study on the global "Pleated Filters Market" for the forecast period of 2026-2033. The report offers a comprehensive analysis of market patterns, growth drivers, competitive landscapes, and regional developments. It segments the market by type, application, and end-user, providing insights into key players like 3M Company, Parker Hannifin Corporation, and Camfil AB.

feed.title: Kroger Brings Zepbound® KwikPen to Retail Pharmacy, Expanding Access and Savings for Self-Pay Patients
feed.summary: The Kroger Co. and The Kroger Family of Pharmacies announced that Zepbound® KwikPen, Eli Lilly's weight-management medication, is now available at participating Kroger pharmacies. This expansion provides a more convenient option for patients, particularly self-pay individuals who may be eligible for Lilly's savings program, reducing out-of-pocket costs. Kroger also offers comprehensive support services, including pharmacist counseling, virtual nutrition care, and access to healthy food options, to promote long-term health outcomes for GLP-1 patients.

feed.title: Coca-Cola tops growth factor grades among S&P consumer staples holdings (KO:NYSE)
feed.summary: Coca-Cola leads the S&P consumer staples sector in growth factor grades, according to an analysis amid broader market challenges. This ranking provides insights into resilient stocks within defensives during periods of geopolitical tension and market shifts. The article highlights the top 10 consumer staples holdings.

feed.title: News | Hotel leaders see deals market opening up
feed.summary: Hotel industry leaders are expressing increased optimism about the deals market opening up in 2026, with many anticipating more transactions. Executives from publicly traded companies like Host Hotels & Resorts, Pebblebrook Hotel Trust, and Hyatt Hotels Corp. discussed recent sales and potential opportunities, noting improved debt markets, increased buyer interest, and a focus on growth and portfolio optimization. Several companies highlighted their strategies of divesting non-core assets to reinvest in higher-return opportunities or return capital to shareholders.

feed.title: Salesforce launches $25 billion share repurchase funded by senior notes issuance
feed.summary: Salesforce has priced a $25 billion senior notes offering to fund an accelerated share repurchase of $25 billion in common stock. The company views this move as a signal that its stock is undervalued, despite concerns about AI disruption and flat performance. This strategy aims to leverage low interest rates to boost earnings per share and offset dilution from stock-based compensation.

feed.title: Are Charles River’s New Advanced Therapy Deals Quietly Rewriting Its Drug-Development Moat Story (CRL)?
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories (CRL) recently announced collaborations with Locus Cell and Therna Biosciences, deepening its involvement in advanced therapies like cell, gene, and RNA platforms. These partnerships could strengthen CRL's position in complex drug development, particularly in ultra-rare diseases, offering higher-value testing opportunities but also increasing exposure to project delays. While these deals support the long-term advanced therapies narrative, they don't immediately resolve near-term pressures of flat revenue guidance and funding constraints, and rising non-animal methods could reshape CRL's core business.
